assertions.text: Correctly identifies the system as high-risk via Annex I Path A (Art. 6(1)) because Regulation 2017/745 (Medical Devices) is listed in Annex I Section A
assertions.evidence: Response mislabels the classification as 'Annex III, Point 5(a)' (which is actually about public authority benefits assessment) rather than Art. 6(1) / Annex I Path A. While it does note EU MDR is in Annex I of the AI Act, it does not correctly identify Art. 6(1) as the governing provision.

assertions.text: Explains that MDR CE marking does NOT automatically satisfy AI Act compliance requirements
assertions.evidence: Response explicitly states 'the existing CE marking under EU MDR does not fully satisfy EU AI Act requirements' and lists gaps including AI-specific transparency and logging requirements

assertions.text: Explains that AI Act requirements (Arts. 9-17) must be integrated into the existing MDR conformity assessment procedure
assertions.evidence: Response references 'Article 8(1)' fast-track pathway and the integration mechanism where AI Act requirements are folded into the existing sectoral conformity procedure

assertions.text: States the 2 August 2027 deadline specifically for Annex I safety component systems
assertions.evidence: Response states 'High-risk Annex I systems (products under harmonisation law): 2 August 2027' in its timeline table, even if the conclusion expresses uncertainty about which deadline applies

assertions.text: Identifies hospital deployer obligations under Art. 26 (instructions compliance, staff competence, monitoring, log retention)
assertions.evidence: Response has a dedicated 'Deployer Obligations Under Article 26' section covering human oversight, FRIA, log retention, monitoring, and registration in the EU database
